rationale: The citing paper explicitly emphasizes prior expectations as a key moderator of assortment-density effects and positions itself against prior choice-overload/assortment-size research, while the cited paper is a core assortment-size study centered on expectation-based mechanisms (expectation/disconfirmation). Given this conceptual overlap (assortment size/density and expectations) and a confirmed DOI-level match, this citation is plausibly substantive rather than an incidental mention.
